name: Message-Oriented Programming

corePrinciples: Components communicate only by sending messages
No shared mutable state across components
x.controlDataFlow: Asynchronous (or synchronous) messages travel between isolated components; receivers decide how to react.
x.suitableProjects: Distributed systems, concurrent components, GUI event handling, and service integration.

def: A style where components interact solely by exchanging messages rather than sharing state or calling each other directly.
problem: Shared state and direct calls couple components tightly and create concurrency hazards.
mechanism: Senders enqueue messages; receivers process them from a mailbox/queue/channel on their own schedule. Decoupling in space (who) and time (when) follows naturally; underlies actors and many integration patterns.
failures: Hidden temporal coupling via message ordering assumptions
myths: "Message passing removes all races" — protocol-level ordering bugs remain
